title: Workflow improvements
excerpt:
Suggestions for workflows to improve: 

Include condition for if date is today (or a variable date), yes or no
Able to copy and paste Actions or entire paths
When changing the trigger of a workflow, the entire automation…

posts.author: joshryan (Hosh Ryan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Suggestions for workflows to improve:

  1. Include condition for if date is today (or a variable date), yes or no
  2. Able to copy and paste Actions or entire paths
  3. When changing the trigger of a workflow, the entire automations Item ID variable receives an error that needs to be replaced. Please fix.
  4. Match (not if yes or no) for people columns

posts.author: jbreen3ki (JJ Breen)
posts.cookedHtml:
I totally agree with #1, and not sure why it wasn’t included.

I’ve put in my own separate request for item #2, so I’ve added a vote to this request as well.

Item #3 is incredibly frustrating. It makes duplicating a workflow for reuse extremely frustrating and practically pointless.

I haven’t experienced #4 yet myself.

Great suggestions, @joshryan , you have my vote!

posts.author: FdL (Franck de Luca)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

I’d like to add one small change to that list that would help a lot:
When you create an action on a status, you do not know which status it is

image479×219 8.5 KB

You have to open it to be able to know what status is set up. Or you have to modify manually the name of the step like this

image440×133 6.55 KB

It would be very nice and effective if monday could automatically put somewhere on the step what status is changing.

Thank you

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve written about this in a separate link somewhere. I have so many more ideas as well.

You can now copy/paste. It’s a bit of an annoying use, but it is possible.

I’d love some consistency in some of the people stuff. Additionally, I’d like some conditional stuff such as:

Find connected item —> No connected item then do x.
Find matching item → No matching item, then do x.

That type of stuff.

Additionally:
Formula blocks in workflows
Using formulas & mirrors to copy over into Text/Status/Numbers columns.
Using formulas for if conditions

And a lot more. I feel like I keep adding, lol.

See below.

Workflows are amazing! Platform discussions
God bless workflows!!! They aren’t 100% perfect yet, but I’m sure many of the below feature requests for workflows will be coming soon down the pipeline. Although some of my workflows span over 250 blocks, it still saves me more time than learning how to use Make or Zapier. Some of the things that have been really bothering me are fixed in workflows, so kudos to creating workflows!!! Some rooms for improvement: Copy/Paste Blocks monday doc automations integrations with other platforms easier…
workflow block responses during creation and/or during run activity Feature requests
I’d love the ability to see workflow block responses either during the activity run log OR while I’m creating the workflow so I know whether the workflow would work or is responding correctly.
Formulas in workflows Feature requests
The new workflows have been immensely helpful in many of my processes. However, I need to be able to utilize formulas for a multitude of reasons. Having the ability to either reference a formula column, or even see create formula blocks to be referenced in other blocks in workflows would be huge!!!

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just saw that monday added the conditional find matching item. Kudos to monday!!!

Now I have to fix ALL my workflows.

Screenshot 2025-07-20 at 14.30.28621×591 20.1 KB

posts.author: jhonnmonak (Jhonn Monak)
posts.cookedHtml: To improve workflows, add support for dynamic date conditions like “if date is today,” allow copying/pasting of actions or full paths for faster automation setup, and ensure that changing a trigger doesn’t break the Item ID variable—automatically remap it instead. Also, enhance people column logic by enabling direct matching beyond just yes/no conditions.
